name: Orange-Raisin Muffins
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 400°F. Line a 12-cup muffin pan with paper liners or spray the bottoms only.
In a medium bowl, combine flour, wheat germ, raisins, granulated sugar, baking powder and salt; mix well.
In a small bowl, combine milk, oil, egg, grated orange peel until blended. Add to flour mixture, stirring just until dry ingredients are moistened.
Fill muffin cups 2/3 full with batter. Bake 18 to 20 minutes, or until light golden brown and a wooden toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean. Remove muffins to a wire rack to cool.
In a small bowl, combine confectioners' sugar and orange juice. Drizzle over slightly cooled muffins. Serve warm.
NOTES: To freeze, wrap muffins securely in foil or place them in a freezer bag. Seal, label, and freeze. To reheat frozen muffins, unwrap; microwave at High (100% power) about 30 seconds per muffin.
Per serving (1 muffin): About 200 calories, 5 grams protein, 32 grams carbs, 5 grams fat, 22% of calories from fat, 0 milligrams cholesterol, 140 milligrams sodium.
